items.project_definition: The primary interface exposing endpoints under /api/v1/ and returning structured data envelopes.
The set of conditional behavioral tests used to verify that a feature meets its defined success criteria.
A learning offering owned by an instructor containing a title, description, price in cents, published status, and ordered modules.
The online learning platform enabling instructional content management and student progress tracking.
The latent mechanism required to securely store the password credential mentioned in the account identity.
A relationship between a student and a learning offering that records a completion date and progress percentage.
Specific system mandates that define the necessary behaviors to implement user stories.
The isolated version control stream identified as 001-coursehub-api for developing this specific set of capabilities.
The requirement to handle pricing as an integer value in cents to avoid floating point inaccuracies.
The assumed international standard for representing the completion date recorded during learning progress.
A standalone verification procedure used to confirm that a specific user journey can be completed successfully.
The raw user description providing the initial logic for the online learning platform's requirements.
The lightweight data-interchange format used for the API responses' data, meta, and errors envelopes.
A child record of a learning offering representing a lesson or section within a fixed sequence.
The architectural style used for the network endpoints to ensure stateless communication between the client and server.
Quantifiable metrics used to determine if the implemented functionality meets the project goals.
A high-level description of a feature from the perspective of an end-user, used to drive implementation.
A shared account identity containing a role, email, password credential, and display name.
